text: Sentinel lymph node section from left axilla, H&E stained, 20x magnification. Dense cellular region visible in the subcapsular sinus area with irregular nuclear morphology.
Lymph node tissue section showing predominantly normal germinal centers with reactive follicular hyperplasia. No obvious atypical cells at scanning magnification.
Section from axillary lymph node with a small cluster of atypical cells in the peripheral sinus. Cells show high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio and pleomorphic nuclei.
Whole-slide image of sentinel lymph node with extensive tissue folding artifact in the upper-right quadrant. Remaining tissue appears unremarkable with intact cortex and medulla.
Lymph node section showing a large area of macrometastasis replacing approximately 40% of the nodal architecture. Tumor cells arranged in glandular patterns consistent with ductal carcinoma.
High-power field of subcapsular sinus showing isolated tumor cells (ITC) scattered among macrophages and lymphocytes. Immunohistochemistry recommended for confirmation.
Unremarkable lymph node with normal cortical and paracortical architecture. Sinusoidal histiocytosis present but no evidence of metastatic disease.
Lymph node section with micrometastasis measuring approximately 1.5 mm in the subcapsular region. Tumor cells show moderate nuclear atypia with occasional mitotic figures.
Section showing poor fixation artifact with tissue retraction and variable staining intensity. Focal areas of crush artifact near the capsule complicate interpretation.
Sentinel lymph node with multiple foci of metastatic carcinoma involving both subcapsular and parenchymal regions. Extranodal extension noted at the hilum.
